relationships.description: ISO 31000 Risk Assessment process maps to NIST CSF Risk Assessment category. Both address identifying, analyzing, and evaluating risks.
ISO 31000 Risk Identification maps to NIST CSF Vulnerability Identification. Both focus on finding potential risks/vulnerabilities.
ISO 31000 Scope, context and criteria relates to NIST CSF Asset Management. Understanding context includes knowing what assets exist.
ISO 31000 Communication and consultation relates to NIST CSF Incident Response Communication. Both emphasize stakeholder communication.
ISO 31000 Monitoring and review maps to NIST CSF Continuous Monitoring. Both address ongoing surveillance of risk environment.
ISO 31000 Risk Treatment maps to NIST CSF Protect function. Risk treatment includes implementing protective safeguards.
ISO 31000 Continual improvement principle maps to NIST CSF Improvement category. Both emphasize ongoing enhancement.
ISO 31000 Leadership and commitment maps to NIST CSF Govern function. Both address organizational governance of risk management.
ISO 31000 Framework Design relates to NIST CSF Risk Management Strategy. Both involve establishing risk management approaches.
ISO 31010 Brainstorming technique supports ISO 31000 Risk Identification process step.
ISO 31010 Checklists technique supports ISO 31000 Risk Identification process step.
ISO 31010 FMEA technique supports ISO 31000 Risk Analysis process step.
ISO 31010 Fault Tree Analysis supports ISO 31000 Risk Analysis process step.
ISO 31010 Event Tree Analysis supports ISO 31000 Risk Analysis process step.
ISO 31010 Bow-tie Analysis supports ISO 31000 Risk Assessment process.
ISO 31010 Root Cause Analysis supports ISO 31000 Risk Analysis process step.
ISO 31010 Monte Carlo simulation supports ISO 31000 Risk Analysis process step.
ISO 31010 HAZOP supports ISO 31000 Risk Identification process step.
ISO 31010 Delphi technique implements ISO 31000 Inclusive principle through expert consultation.
ISO 31010 SWOT Analysis supports ISO 31000 Scope, context and criteria process step.
ISO 31010 Cost-benefit analysis supports ISO 31000 Risk Treatment process step.
ISO 31010 Multi-criteria decision analysis supports ISO 31000 Risk Evaluation process step.
ISO 31010 Business Impact Analysis supports ISO 31000 Risk Analysis process step.
ISO 31010 Business Impact Analysis supports NIST CSF Recover function planning.
ISO 31010 Root Cause Analysis supports NIST CSF Incident Analysis category.
ISO 31010 Bow-tie Analysis supports NIST CSF Protect function by identifying barriers and controls.
NIST CSF Organizational Context relates to ISO 31000 Framework Design. Both involve understanding organizational context.
NIST CSF Adverse Event Analysis relates to ISO 31000 Monitoring and review process step.
NIST CSF Incident Mitigation relates to ISO 31000 Risk Treatment. Incident mitigation is a form of risk treatment.
NIST CSF Data Security implements ISO 31000 Risk Treatment for data-related risks.
NIST CSF Identity Management and Access Control implements ISO 31000 Risk Treatment for access-related risks.
ISO 9000 Planning for QMS addresses risks and opportunities, mapping to ISO 31000 Risk Management Framework Design.
ISO 9000 Improvement principle maps to ISO 31000 Continual improvement principle.
ISO 9000 Process approach relates to ISO 31000 Structured and comprehensive principle.
ISO 9000 Evidence-based decision making maps to ISO 31000 Best available information principle.
ISO 27000 Operation (information security risk assessment) implements ISO 31000 Risk Assessment process.
ISO 27000 Planning (actions to address risks) maps to ISO 31000 Risk Management Framework Design.
ISO 27000 Improvement maps to ISO 31000 Framework Improvement, both emphasizing continual enhancement.
ISO 27000 ISMS implements NIST CSF Govern function for information security.
ISO 27000 Information Security Controls implement NIST CSF Protect function.
ISO 27000 Physical controls implement NIST CSF Protect function.
ISO 27000 Technological controls implement NIST CSF Data Security category.
ISO 27000 ISMS and ISO 9000 QMS are both management system standards with similar structures.
FMN Federated Identity Management implements ISO 27000 Technological controls for identity and access.
FMN Multilevel security principle relates to ISO 27000 ISMS security requirements.
FMN Federated Identity Management implements NIST CSF Identity Management and Access Control.
FMN Information Sharing Agreements implement NIST CSF Data Security for shared information.
FMN Information Sharing Agreements implement ISO 31000 Risk Treatment for information sharing risks.
Data Centric data-level protection implements ISO 27000 Technological controls.
Data Centric encryption implements NIST CSF Data Security category.
Data awareness (knowing what data you have) implements ISO 31000 Scope and context establishment.
Data discovery and inventory implements NIST CSF Asset Management for data assets.
Data classification implements ISO 27000 Organizational controls for information classification.
Zero Trust least privilege access implements ISO 27000 Technological controls for access management.
Zero Trust verify explicitly principle implements NIST CSF Identity Management and Access Control.
Zero Trust device monitoring implements NIST CSF Continuous Monitoring.
Zero Trust assume breach principle relates to ISO 31000 Dynamic principle - both recognize changing threat landscape.
Zero Trust Policy Enforcement Point can implement FMN Federated Identity Management.
Zero Trust dynamic policy maps to Data Centric policy-driven access - both embed policy with data/resources.
Zero Trust continuous authentication implements ISO 27000 Performance evaluation requirements.
Data Centric persistent protection supports Zero Trust secure communication requirements.
NIST SP 800-53 Access Control family implements NIST CSF Identity Management, Authentication and Access Control category.
NIST SP 800-53 Access Control family maps to ISO 27001 Technological controls for access management.
NIST SP 800-53 Access Control family implements Zero Trust least privileged access principle.
NIST SP 800-53 Awareness and Training family implements NIST CSF Protect function through security awareness.
NIST SP 800-53 Awareness and Training family maps to ISO 27001 People controls for security awareness.
NIST SP 800-53 Audit and Accountability family implements NIST CSF Continuous Monitoring through audit logging.
NIST SP 800-53 Audit and Accountability family maps to ISO 27001 Performance evaluation through audit records.
NIST SP 800-53 Assessment, Authorization, and Monitoring family implements NIST CSF Risk Assessment category.
NIST SP 800-53 Assessment, Authorization, and Monitoring family implements NIST CSF Risk Management Strategy.
NIST SP 800-53 Assessment, Authorization, and Monitoring family implements ISO 31000 Risk Assessment process.
NIST SP 800-53 Configuration Management family implements NIST CSF Data Security through configuration baselines.
NIST SP 800-53 Configuration Management family implements NIST CSF Platform Security through secure configurations.
NIST SP 800-53 Contingency Planning family implements NIST CSF Recover function through business continuity plans.
NIST SP 800-53 Contingency Planning family implements NIST CSF Technology Infrastructure Resilience.
NIST SP 800-53 Contingency Planning family implements ISO 31000 Risk Treatment through continuity planning.
NIST SP 800-53 Identification and Authentication family implements NIST CSF Identity Management and Access Control.
NIST SP 800-53 Identification and Authentication family implements Zero Trust verify explicitly principle.
NIST SP 800-53 Incident Response family implements NIST CSF Respond function.
NIST SP 800-53 Incident Response family maps to ISO 27001 Operation requirements for incident handling.
NIST SP 800-53 Maintenance family implements NIST CSF Protect function through controlled system maintenance.
NIST SP 800-53 Media Protection family implements NIST CSF Data Security through media handling controls.
NIST SP 800-53 Physical and Environmental Protection family implements NIST CSF Protect function.
NIST SP 800-53 Physical and Environmental Protection family maps to ISO 27001 Physical controls.
NIST SP 800-53 Planning family implements NIST CSF Risk Management Strategy through security planning.
NIST SP 800-53 Planning family implements ISO 31000 Risk Management Framework Design.
NIST SP 800-53 Program Management family implements NIST CSF Govern function through organizational security governance.
NIST SP 800-53 Program Management family implements ISO 31000 Leadership and commitment.
NIST SP 800-53 Program Management family maps to ISO 27001 ISMS management system framework.
NIST SP 800-53 Personnel Security family implements NIST CSF Protect function through personnel screening and management.
NIST SP 800-53 Personnel Security family maps to ISO 27001 People controls.
NIST SP 800-53 PII Processing and Transparency family implements NIST CSF Govern function for privacy governance.
NIST SP 800-53 Risk Assessment family implements NIST CSF Risk Assessment category.
NIST SP 800-53 Risk Assessment family implements ISO 31000 Risk Assessment process.
NIST SP 800-53 System and Services Acquisition family implements NIST CSF Organizational Context for supply chain awareness.
NIST SP 800-53 System and Services Acquisition family maps to ISO 27001 Organizational controls for supplier management.
NIST SP 800-53 System and Communications Protection family implements NIST CSF Data Security through encryption and boundary protection.
NIST SP 800-53 System and Communications Protection family maps to ISO 27001 Technological controls.
NIST SP 800-53 System and Communications Protection family implements Data Centric data-level protection principles.
NIST SP 800-53 System and Information Integrity family implements NIST CSF Detect function through integrity monitoring.
NIST SP 800-53 System and Information Integrity family implements NIST CSF Adverse Event Analysis.
NIST SP 800-53 Supply Chain Risk Management family implements ISO 31000 Risk Treatment for supply chain risks.
NIST SP 800-53 Supply Chain Risk Management family implements NIST CSF Organizational Context for supply chain governance.
NIST SP 800-53 Supply Chain Risk Management relates to FMN Multilevel security for coalition supply chain assurance.
NIST RMF Prepare step maps to NIST CSF Govern function. Both establish organizational risk management strategy, roles, and risk tolerance.
NIST RMF Risk Management Strategy task maps to NIST CSF Risk Management Strategy category.
NIST RMF organization-level Risk Assessment task maps to NIST CSF Risk Assessment category.
NIST RMF Continuous Monitoring Strategy task maps to NIST CSF Continuous Monitoring category.
NIST RMF Categorize step maps to NIST CSF Identify function. Categorizing systems by impact aligns with understanding organizational cybersecurity risks.
NIST RMF System Description task maps to NIST CSF Asset Management. Documenting system characteristics is a form of asset management.
NIST RMF Select step maps to NIST SP 800-53 Planning family. RMF Select drives the selection and tailoring of 800-53 controls.
NIST RMF Select step relates to NIST CSF Protect function. Selecting controls provides the safeguards that implement protection.
NIST RMF Implement step maps to NIST CSF Protect function. Implementing controls directly provides the protective safeguards.
NIST RMF Assess step maps to NIST SP 800-53 Assessment, Authorization, and Monitoring family.
NIST RMF Authorize step maps to NIST SP 800-53 Assessment, Authorization, and Monitoring family for authorization decisions.
NIST RMF Monitor step maps to NIST CSF Detect function. Ongoing monitoring enables detection of adverse events.
NIST RMF Monitor step relates to NIST CSF Improvement category. Continuous monitoring drives identification of improvements.
NIST RMF Prepare step maps to ISO 31000 Framework Design. Both establish the organizational context and approach for risk management.
NIST RMF Risk Management Roles task relates to ISO 31000 Leadership and commitment. Assigning roles requires leadership engagement.
NIST RMF Categorize step maps to ISO 31000 Scope, context and criteria. Categorization establishes the scope and context for risk management.
NIST RMF Risk Analysis and Determination task maps to ISO 31000 Risk Analysis process step.
NIST RMF Risk Response task maps to ISO 31000 Risk Treatment. Both address selecting and implementing risk response options.
NIST RMF Monitor step maps to ISO 31000 Monitoring and review process step.
NIST RMF Assess step maps to ISO 27000 Performance evaluation. Both involve assessing the effectiveness of security controls.
NIST RMF Organizationally-Tailored Control Baselines maps to NIST SP 800-53 Program Management family for baseline establishment.
EU AI Act risk management system requirement for high-risk AI implements ISO 31000 Risk Assessment process for AI-specific risks.
EU AI Act risk management system relates to NIST RMF Prepare step. Both require establishing risk management before system deployment.
EU AI Act accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity requirement implements NIST CSF Protect function for AI system resilience.
EU AI Act cybersecurity requirement relates to NIST SP 800-53 System and Information Integrity family for AI system integrity.
EU AI Act record-keeping (logging) requirement implements NIST CSF Continuous Monitoring through automated event logging.
EU AI Act logging requirement maps to NIST SP 800-53 Audit and Accountability family.
EU AI Act human oversight requirement implements ISO 31000 Monitoring and review through human-in-the-loop processes.
EU AI Act data governance requirement relates to ISO 27000 Organizational controls for data management practices.
EU AI Act data governance relates to Data Centric data awareness principle. Understanding training data quality requires data awareness.
EU AI Act conformity assessment maps to NIST RMF Assess step. Both involve formal assessment of controls/requirements compliance.
EU AI Act conformity assessment relates to NIST RMF Authorize step. Both require formal approval before system operation.
EU AI Act provider obligations (quality management system) relate to ISO 9000 Quality Management System requirements.
EU AI Act risk management system implements ISO 31000 Risk Treatment through adoption of suitable risk management measures for AI systems.
EU AI Act Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment implements ISO 31000 Risk Evaluation for fundamental rights impacts.
EU AI Act transparency requirements relate to NIST CSF Govern function. Transparency supports organizational governance of AI systems.
EU AI Act critical infrastructure high-risk area relates to NIST CSF Technology Infrastructure Resilience.
EU AI Act systemic risk obligations for GPAI relate to ISO 31000 Dynamic principle. Systemic AI risks require dynamic, evolving risk assessment.
EU AI Act deployer obligations (monitoring operation) relate to NIST RMF Monitor step for ongoing system oversight.
EU AI Act biometrics high-risk area relates to NIST CSF Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control.
EU AI Act governance framework relates to ISO 27000 ISMS. Both establish institutional frameworks for managing technology-related risks and compliance.
ISO 23894 AI risk management principles implement ISO 31000 principles, extending them to the AI context.
ISO 23894 AI risk management process implements the ISO 31000 risk management process for AI systems.
ISO 23894 AI risk assessment implements ISO 31000 Risk Assessment with AI-specific risk identification, analysis, and evaluation.
ISO 23894 AI risk treatment implements ISO 31000 Risk Treatment with AI-specific controls and mitigations.
ISO 23894 AI monitoring and review implements ISO 31000 Monitoring and review for AI system performance and emerging risks.
ISO 23894 AI risk management process maps to NIST AI RMF core functions. Both provide structured approaches to AI risk management.
ISO 23894 AI risk identification maps to NIST AI RMF Map function for identifying AI risks in context.
ISO 23894 AI risk analysis maps to NIST AI RMF Measure function for assessing and analyzing AI risks.
ISO 23894 AI risk treatment maps to NIST AI RMF Manage function for prioritizing and acting on AI risks.
ISO 23894 AI risk assessment guidance supports ISO 42001 AI risk assessment requirement. ISO 23894 provides the methodology that ISO 42001 requires.
ISO 23894 AI risk treatment guidance supports ISO 42001 AI risk treatment requirement.
ISO 23894 data-related risks relate to EU AI Act data governance requirement. Both address data quality, bias, and representativeness for AI.
ISO 23894 model-related risks relate to EU AI Act accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity requirement.
ISO 23894 use-related risks (over-reliance, automation bias) relate to EU AI Act human oversight requirement.
ISO 23894 AI lifecycle considerations map to NIST AI RMF AI lifecycle stages.
ISO 23894 societal risks (discrimination) relate to GDPR special categories of personal data, as AI discrimination often involves processing sensitive data.
ISO 23894 system-related risks (security vulnerabilities) relate to MITRE ATLAS AI attack tactics.
ISO 23894 scope, context and criteria relates to NIST CSF Identify function for understanding AI system context.
ISO 42001 AIMS relates to ISO 27000 ISMS. Both follow the ISO Harmonized Structure for management systems with compatible requirements.
ISO 42001 AI risk assessment implements ISO 31000 Risk Assessment process for AI-specific risks.
ISO 42001 AI risk treatment implements ISO 31000 Risk Treatment for AI systems.
ISO 42001 AI policy relates to NIST CSF Govern function for organizational AI governance.
ISO 42001 Leadership clause maps to NIST AI RMF Govern function. Both establish organizational AI risk governance.
ISO 42001 AI system impact assessment relates to EU AI Act Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment.
ISO 42001 AI risk assessment maps to EU AI Act risk management system requirement for high-risk AI.
ISO 42001 AIMS relates to ISO 9000 QMS. Both are ISO management system standards with compatible structures.
ISO 42001 monitoring and measurement implements ISO 31000 Monitoring and review for AI systems.
ISO 42001 continual improvement implements ISO 31000 Continual improvement principle for AIMS.
ISO 42001 understanding organizational context maps to NIST AI RMF Map function for understanding AI system context.
ISO 42001 internal audit maps to ISO 27000 Performance evaluation. Both require systematic assessment of management system effectiveness.
ISO 42001 AI system impact assessment relates to GDPR DPIA. AI systems processing personal data require both AI impact and privacy impact assessments.
ISO 42001 Leadership clause relates to Google SAIF Unified AI Security Governance for consistent AI governance.
NIST AI RMF Govern function maps to NIST CSF Govern function. Both address organizational governance of risk management.
NIST AI RMF Govern function maps to ISO 31000 Leadership and commitment for AI risk governance.
NIST AI RMF Map function maps to ISO 31000 Scope, context and criteria. Both establish context for risk identification.
NIST AI RMF Map function maps to NIST CSF Identify function. Both focus on understanding risks in context.
NIST AI RMF Measure function maps to ISO 31000 Risk Assessment. Both involve assessing and analyzing identified risks.
NIST AI RMF Measure function relates to NIST CSF Detect. Measuring AI risks includes detecting adverse events and anomalies.
NIST AI RMF Manage function maps to ISO 31000 Risk Treatment. Both prioritize and act on identified risks.
NIST AI RMF Manage function maps to NIST CSF Respond. Both include incident response and recovery planning.
NIST AI RMF Secure and Resilient characteristic maps to NIST CSF Protect function.
NIST AI RMF Privacy-Enhanced characteristic relates to GDPR data protection principles.
NIST AI RMF Fairness characteristic relates to EU AI Act prohibited practices. Both address harmful bias and discrimination.
NIST AI RMF Govern function maps to EU AI Act risk management system requirement. Both establish AI risk governance.
NIST AI RMF Explainability characteristic maps to EU AI Act transparency requirement for high-risk AI.
NIST AI RMF legal and regulatory compliance relates to EU AI Act governance and enforcement framework.
NIST AI RMF Third-Party Risk Management relates to NIST SP 800-53 Supply Chain Risk Management family.
NIST AI RMF Map function relates to NIST RMF Categorize step. Both involve understanding system context and classifying risk.
NIST AI RMF Manage function relates to NIST RMF Monitor step. Both involve ongoing risk response and tracking.
Google SAIF Element 1 (Expand security foundations) maps to NIST CSF Protect function.
Google SAIF AI Supply Chain Security relates to NIST SP 800-53 Supply Chain Risk Management.
Google SAIF Element 2 (Detection and response) maps to NIST CSF Detect function.
Google SAIF AI Incident Response maps to NIST CSF Respond function.
Google SAIF AI Red Teaming relates to MITRE ATLAS tactics. Red teaming exercises simulate ATLAS-documented attack techniques.
Google SAIF Element 3 (Automate defenses) relates to NIST CSF Continuous Monitoring.
Google SAIF Element 4 (Harmonize platform controls) maps to NIST CSF Govern function for consistent governance.
Google SAIF Unified AI Security Governance maps to NIST AI RMF Govern function.
Google SAIF Element 5 (Adapt controls) maps to NIST AI RMF Measure function. Both involve continuous evaluation and feedback.
Google SAIF Element 6 (Contextualize risks) maps to NIST AI RMF Map function. Both contextualize AI risks in business processes.
Google SAIF Business-Contextualized AI Risk Assessment implements ISO 31000 Scope, context and criteria.
Google SAIF Human Oversight relates to EU AI Act human oversight requirement for high-risk AI.
Google SAIF Element 1 (Expand security foundations) relates to ISO 27000 ISMS for foundational security management.
MITRE ATLAS Reconnaissance tactic relates to NIST CSF Identify function. Understanding reconnaissance threats informs asset identification.
MITRE ATLAS Defense Evasion tactic relates to NIST CSF Detect function. Detection must account for AI-specific evasion techniques.
MITRE ATLAS Impact tactic relates to NIST CSF Respond function. AI system impacts require incident response.
MITRE ATLAS ML Attack Staging relates to NIST AI RMF Secure and Resilient characteristic. AI systems must be resilient against these attacks.
MITRE ATLAS Data Poisoning technique relates to EU AI Act data governance requirement. Data governance must address poisoning risks.
MITRE ATLAS ML Supply Chain Compromise relates to NIST SP 800-53 Supply Chain Risk Management.
MITRE ATLAS Model Extraction relates to EU AI Act accuracy and cybersecurity requirement. Systems must be resilient against extraction attacks.
MITRE ATLAS Prompt Injection relates to NIST AI RMF Safety characteristic. Prompt injection undermines AI system safety.
MITRE ATLAS Exfiltration tactics relate to ISO 27000 Technological controls for preventing data and model theft.
MITRE ATLAS Erode Guardrails technique relates to Google SAIF Adaptive Risk Mitigation. Guardrail evasion requires adaptive controls.
NIS2 risk management measures map to NIST CSF Protect function. Both establish cybersecurity safeguards.
NIS2 risk analysis requirement implements ISO 31000 Risk Assessment process.
NIS2 incident handling requirement maps to NIST CSF Respond function.
NIS2 business continuity requirement maps to NIST CSF Recover function.
NIS2 supply chain security requirement maps to NIST SP 800-53 Supply Chain Risk Management family.
NIS2 cryptography and encryption requirement implements NIST CSF Data Security category.
NIS2 multi-factor authentication requirement implements NIST CSF Identity Management and Access Control.
NIS2 HR security and access control maps to NIST SP 800-53 Access Control family.
NIS2 incident reporting obligations map to NIST CSF Incident Response Reporting and Communication.
NIS2 governance structure relates to ISO 27000 ISMS. NIS2 compliance benefits from an established ISMS.
NIS2 acquisition security requirement maps to NIST CSF Platform Security for secure system acquisition and maintenance.
NIS2 effectiveness assessment requirement implements ISO 31000 Monitoring and review process.
NIS2 cyber hygiene and training requirement maps to NIST SP 800-53 Awareness and Training family.
NIS2 management body accountability maps to NIST CSF Govern function for organizational cybersecurity governance.
NIS2 coordinated vulnerability disclosure relates to NIST SP 800-53 System and Information Integrity.
NIS2 essential entities relate to CER critical sectors. NIS2 and CER cover overlapping sectors with complementary requirements.
NIS2 essential entities in digital infrastructure relate to CER digital infrastructure sector.
DORA ICT Risk Management maps to NIST CSF Protect function. Both establish protective measures for ICT systems.
DORA ICT governance requirement maps to NIST CSF Govern function.
DORA ICT identification requirement maps to NIST CSF Identify function for asset and risk identification.
DORA ICT detection requirement maps to NIST CSF Detect function.
DORA ICT response and recovery maps to NIST CSF Respond function.
DORA ICT response and recovery maps to NIST CSF Recover function.
DORA learning and evolving requirement maps to NIST CSF Improvement category.
DORA ICT risk management framework implements ISO 31000 Framework Design.
DORA incident reporting relates to NIS2 incident reporting. Both impose mandatory incident notification for financial and essential entities respectively.
DORA ICT third-party risk management maps to NIST SP 800-53 Supply Chain Risk Management family.
DORA third-party risk management relates to NIS2 supply chain security. Both address ICT supply chain risks.
DORA threat-led penetration testing relates to NIST SP 800-53 Assessment, Authorization, and Monitoring.
DORA ICT protection requirement implements ISO 27000 Technological controls.
DORA information sharing arrangements relate to NIS2 cybersecurity information sharing.
DORA incident classification relates to NIST SP 800-53 Incident Response family.
DORA ICT risk management implements ISO 27000 Operation requirements for financial sector entities.
DORA ICT risk management relates to CER banking sector. DORA provides sector-specific ICT resilience requirements complementing CER.
GDPR integrity and confidentiality principle implements NIST CSF Data Security requirements for personal data.
GDPR security of processing relates to ISO 27000 ISMS. An ISMS supports GDPR security compliance.
GDPR breach notification to supervisory authority maps to NIST CSF Incident Response Reporting and Communication.
GDPR 72h breach notification relates to NIS2 incident reporting. Both impose mandatory notification timelines.
GDPR breach notification relates to DORA incident reporting for financial entities handling personal data.
GDPR Data Protection Impact Assessment implements ISO 31000 Risk Assessment process for privacy risks.
GDPR DPIA relates to EU AI Act Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment. Both require pre-deployment impact assessment.
GDPR data protection by design relates to ISO 27000 Planning requirements for building security into systems.
GDPR right regarding automated decision-making relates to EU AI Act high-risk AI requirements. Both regulate automated decisions affecting individuals.
GDPR accountability principle relates to NIST CSF Govern function for organizational governance of data protection.
GDPR data protection principles relate to NIST AI RMF Privacy-Enhanced characteristic for AI systems processing personal data.
GDPR security of processing relates to NIST SP 800-53 Access Control for protecting personal data access.
GDPR records of processing activities relate to NIST SP 800-53 Audit and Accountability for data processing auditability.
GDPR Data Protection Officer relates to ISO 27000 ISMS governance. DPO role supports ISMS data protection governance.
GDPR data minimisation principle relates to Data Centric data awareness. Understanding what data exists is prerequisite to minimising it.
GDPR integrity and confidentiality relates to Data Centric encryption for protecting personal data at rest and in transit.
CER risk assessment requirement implements ISO 31000 Risk Assessment for critical entity resilience.
CER resilience obligations map to NIST CSF Protect function. Both require protective safeguards.
CER business continuity measures map to NIST CSF Recover function.
CER incident management measures map to NIST CSF Respond function.
CER incident notification relates to NIS2 incident reporting. Both directive require notification of significant incidents.
CER supply chain security measures relate to NIST SP 800-53 Supply Chain Risk Management.
CER employee security measures map to ISO 27000 People controls for personnel security.
CER physical protection measures map to NIST SP 800-53 Physical and Environmental Protection family.
CER digital infrastructure sector relates to NIS2 essential entities. Digital infrastructure operators are subject to both directives.
CER energy sector relates to NIS2 essential entities. Energy operators are subject to both directives.
CER banking sector relates to DORA ICT risk management. Banking entities are subject to CER resilience and DORA ICT resilience.
CER governance and oversight relates to ISO 31000 Leadership and commitment for critical entity resilience governance.
CER national risk assessment implements ISO 31000 Scope, context and criteria at the national level.
CISA ZTMM Identity pillar maps to NIST CSF Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control.
CISA ZTMM Identity pillar maps to NIST SP 800-53 Access Control family.
CISA ZTMM Identity pillar maps to NIST SP 800-53 Identification and Authentication family.
CISA ZTMM Identity pillar implements the Zero Trust principle of verify explicitly.
CISA ZTMM Devices pillar maps to NIST CSF Platform Security for device hardening and compliance.
CISA ZTMM Devices pillar implements Zero Trust device security and compliance verification.
CISA ZTMM Networks pillar maps to NIST SP 800-53 System and Communications Protection family.
CISA ZTMM Networks pillar implements Zero Trust secure communication tenet.
CISA ZTMM Networks pillar implements Zero Trust per-session access tenet for network segmentation.
CISA ZTMM Applications & Workloads pillar maps to NIST SP 800-53 Configuration Management family.
CISA ZTMM Applications & Workloads pillar maps to NIST CSF Platform Security for application hardening.
CISA ZTMM Data pillar maps to NIST CSF Data Security for data protection controls.
CISA ZTMM Data pillar maps to NIST SP 800-53 Media Protection family.
CISA ZTMM Data pillar relates to Data Centric Security principles for data-level protection.
CISA ZTMM Visibility & Analytics maps to NIST CSF Detect function for continuous monitoring.
CISA ZTMM Visibility & Analytics maps to NIST SP 800-53 Audit and Accountability family.
CISA ZTMM Governance maps to NIST CSF Govern function for organizational governance alignment.
CISA ZTMM Governance relates to ISO 31000 Leadership and Commitment for risk governance.
CISA ZTMM Governance relates to ISO 27000 ISMS for information security governance alignment.
